codeReview.summary: The quest completion flow is well-implemented with a comprehensive API endpoint, celebration UI, best work selection, gallery preview, and gallery submission. Tests cover all major flows (23 new tests across API and page). Bilingual translations are complete and consistent. Accessibility is handled with role=status screen reader announcements. The code follows project conventions (Prisma, Zod validation, next-intl, Tailwind, shadcn/ui). There are two non-blocking issues: a hardcoded English string in the UI and the skip-gallery path not explicitly updating quest status.

issues.description: Hardcoded English string 'Day {mission.day}' in the day label overlay on photo thumbnails. This should use a translation key (e.g., t('dayLabel', { day: mission.day })) to support Indonesian locale. All other user-facing strings correctly use next-intl translations, but this one was missed. In the Indonesian view, children will still see 'Day 3' instead of 'Hari 3' or equivalent.

When skipGallery is true, the handler returns immediately without updating quest.status to 'completed'. The gallery-submission path updates quest status in a transaction (line 184-187), but the skip path does not. If the quest was already marked 'completed' by the daily progress feature, this is benign. However, if a quest could reach this endpoint in 'active' status (all missions completed but quest not yet marked completed), skipping would leave it in 'active' status. This is an edge case inconsistency between the two paths.

The feature description specifies 'Redirect to gallery after submission', but the implementation shows a success state with a 'View Gallery' link rather than an automatic redirect. This is arguably a better UX (user sees confirmation before navigating), but differs from the literal requirement. The current approach is acceptable as it still provides a clear path to the gallery.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The page component uses hardcoded 'Day' text on line 450 instead of a translation key. AGENTS.md says 'All user-facing strings MUST use translations - never hardcode text' but this convention was violated in one spot. The rest of the page correctly uses next-intl. This is a minor miss that could be caught by a lint rule or a more specific reminder about checking all JSX text nodes.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/app/[locale]/quest/[id]/complete/page.tsx:450 — `Day {mission.day}` is hardcoded English text inside a JSX element, while the same page correctly uses t() for all other strings.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The API route at /api/quest/[id]/complete does not use sanitizeInput() on any user-provided data. While the only user input is selectedPhotoUrl (a URL validated by Zod) and skipGallery (a boolean), AGENTS.md's security section says 'Sanitize ALL user-generated text UNCONDITIONALLY before rendering or including in AI prompts.' The URL is stored in the database as imageUrl for the gallery entry. Since it's a URL (not free text) and validated by Zod's .url(), this is low risk, but it's a pattern deviation from the project's stated convention.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/app/api/quest/[id]/complete/route.ts — no import of sanitizeInput; compare to src/app/api/quest/generate/route.ts which does import and use sanitizeInput on dream and localContext.

summary: Quest completion flow passes review. Implementation covers all expected behaviors: celebration screen with visual trophy/sparkles, 7-day journey summary, best work photo selection as radio buttons, gallery entry preview with talent/location metadata, gallery submission via POST endpoint with transaction, skip option, duplicate prevention (409), ownership verification, incomplete quest validation, bilingual translations (EN/ID), and accessible screen reader announcements. Two non-blocking issues found: (1) hardcoded 'Day' text in photo thumbnails not using i18n, (2) skip-gallery path doesn't explicitly update quest status to 'completed'. Test coverage is thorough with 23 new tests covering auth, ownership, validation, gallery creation, skip flow, duplicate prevention, UI states, and accessibility.
